summary_one_line: Input must encode a valid protocol/URL structure that drives curl deep enough to invoke Curl_fopen on a save path so the stat/S_ISREG guard is exercised on the T side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A structurally valid protocol request (e.g. a pop3://host:port URL plus mail-style headers) that survives curl's URL/protocol parsing and reaches the cookie/HSTS save code path, which then calls Curl_fopen and takes the T side of the stat()==-1 || !S_ISREG guard.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner seeds contain a parseable scheme+authority (pop3://127.0.0.1:9001/850) and RFC822-style From:/To: headers, giving the well-formed protocol structure needed to reach Curl_fopen and resolve the T side (the cov overlay marks line 64 'goto fail' [W] and the T-side branch at line 59 fires only for W).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are short/degenerate byte runs (e.g. a 10-byte zero-padded blob, or repetitive +./-/% noise) that do not parse into a valid scheme+authority, so curl never drives the save path to the resolving outcome; on the loser side line 59 is hit 51 times on F only and the F-side return CURLE_OK (line 63 [L]) dominates.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer and substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching input offsets, letting it plant the literal scheme/authority and field bytes that curl's parser compares against; naive only has havoc/token mutators with no CMP introspection, so it rarely synthesizes the structured prefix needed to reach the guard's T side.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked T side is the stat/S_ISREG guard inside Curl_fopen.
cmplog beats naive at this branch with the delta attributed to I2S.
Winner seeds carry a parseable protocol URL and mail headers.
Winner seeds include RFC822-style header structure.
Loser seeds are short degenerate byte runs lacking valid structure.
On the winner the function reaches goto fail (the T-side outcome) while the loser returns CURLE_OK on the F-side.
The loser hits line 59 on the F side 51 times while the winner takes the T side; the T side resolves only for W.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: T=2 F=0  T=0 F=51  if(stat(filename, &sb) == -1 || !S_ISREG(sb.st_mode)) {  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes one CMP operand for the other at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: naive has no I2S, only havoc and token mutators.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: **Observed `mutation_op` in seed metadata**: any of the above. No I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 68 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed ac73bec58f30a882` and observed an I2S-floor row (mutation_op = -) at depth 1 of the 2-level chain; tool reports '1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 1', the lower-bound I2S signal under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: If a synthetic harness gates only on a non-comparison structural property (e.g. input length, with no scheme/authority literal that curl byte-compares), cmplog would show no advantage over naive at the stat-guard T side, refuting the I2S-substitution attribution.

weakest_evidence_point: The link from the protocol-prefix bytes to specifically the stat()==-1 T outcome is inferred from the deep call chain; the seeds do not directly show which filename argument reaches Curl_fopen or why stat fails on it.
